evals.prompt: I need to set up a system where customers email support@acme.com and get an automatic reply acknowledging their message. How should I set this up with Resend?
evals.expected_output: Agent navigates to BOTH receiving.md (to receive emails) AND sending/overview.md (to send auto-reply). Should set up inbound first, then sending.
expectations: References both receiving and sending references
Specifies inbound/receiving setup before sending setup
Does not route to only one reference

evals.prompt: I want to build a system that receives customer emails, automatically extracts invoice numbers from the body, looks up the order in our database, and updates the status. It should also auto-reply with the current order status. What references do I need?
evals.expected_output: Acknowledges ambiguity — could be rule-based parsing or AI-driven. Either asks whether this is rule-based or AI, or flags that agent-email-inbox skill is needed if AI is involved.
expectations: Acknowledges ambiguity between rule-based and AI-driven processing
Mentions agent-email-inbox skill or security implications if AI is involved
Does not confidently route to a single reference without caveats

evals.prompt: I need to configure my domain for Resend. How do I do that?
evals.expected_output: Clarifies whether user needs sending domain (DNS/SPF/DKIM/DMARC) or receiving domain (MX records), since setup differs.
expectations: Asks for clarification or explains that domain setup differs between sending and receiving
Does not assume sending or receiving without asking

evals.prompt: I need to set up webhooks for Resend. Can you help?
evals.expected_output: Clarifies the use case — delivery status webhooks vs received email webhooks vs agent inbox webhooks.
expectations: Asks for clarification about which type of webhook the user needs
Mentions at least two types: delivery status events and received email events
Does not assume one type of webhook without asking

evals.prompt: When someone emails helpdesk@myapp.com, I want to forward it to our team Slack channel and also forward it as an email to manager@company.com with any attachments. Which Resend references do I need?
evals.expected_output: Both receiving.md (to receive the email) and sending/overview.md (to forward as email). Notes that forwarding with attachments requires single sends, not batch.
expectations: References both receiving and sending
Notes that attachments require single sends (batch doesn't support attachments)
Identifies that receiving the email is a prerequisite to forwarding it

evals.prompt: Customers will email returns@shop.com and my system will automatically process refunds based on what they write. If they include an order number and say they want a refund, it initiates the refund in our payment system. What do you recommend?
evals.expected_output: Flags security concerns — untrusted external senders triggering financial actions. Recommends agent-email-inbox skill or at minimum highlights the risk.
expectations: Flags security risk of untrusted input triggering financial actions
Recommends agent-email-inbox skill or highlights need for security measures
Does not treat this as simple email receiving plus database lookup

evals.prompt: I want to send a monthly newsletter to my 10,000 subscribers with unsubscribe links and engagement tracking using Resend.
evals.expected_output: Notes this is a marketing/broadcast use case, not transactional sending. Recommends Resend Broadcasts.
expectations: Identifies this as a marketing/broadcast use case
Mentions Resend Broadcasts as the appropriate feature
Does not suggest using batch sending for 10,000 newsletter emails

evals.prompt: I need to send a welcome email when a user signs up. How do I do this with Resend?
evals.expected_output: Simple transactional send. Uses quick send example or navigates to sending/overview.md directly.
expectations: Provides a straightforward single email send approach
Does not over-navigate to multiple references
Does not suggest batch sending or agent-email-inbox

evals.prompt:
I'm building a Node.js service. I need to send emails for these cases:

Case A: User resets their password — send a reset link email with a PDF attachment of their account activity.
Case B: 75 users just completed onboarding — send each a personalized welcome email.
Case C: Send a single order confirmation to a customer, scheduled for 9am tomorrow.
Case D: Send 2 emails — one receipt with a PDF attachment, one shipping notification without attachments. Both to the same customer.

For each case, which endpoint should I use (single or batch) and why?
evals.expected_output: Case A: Single (attachments not supported in batch). Case B: Batch (multiple emails, no attachments, under 100). Case C: Single (scheduling not supported in batch). Case D: Two single sends (attachment email can't be batched).
expectations: Case A uses single send because batch doesn't support attachments
Case B uses batch send
Case C uses single send because batch doesn't support scheduling
Case D uses two single sends or explains attachment email can't be batched

evals.prompt: Write a Node.js function that sends a welcome email to a new user. The function may be called multiple times due to retry logic in our queue. User ID: usr_abc123. Include proper idempotency handling.
evals.expected_output: Idempotency key based on event type + entity ID (e.g., welcome-email/usr_abc123), passed as second argument to resend.emails.send().
expectations: Uses a semantic idempotency key that includes the user ID
Does not use a random/UUID-based key
Passes the idempotency key correctly (not inside the email payload)

evals.prompt: I sent a welcome email with idempotency key 'welcome-email/usr_abc123'. Now marketing wants to A/B test: send the SAME user a slightly different welcome email (different subject line) using the same key. What happens? What should I do?
evals.expected_output: Same key + different payload = 409 error. Must use a new key for the variant.
expectations: Identifies that same key with different payload causes a 409 error
Recommends using a new/different key for the variant
Does not say it will overwrite the first email

evals.expected_output: Resend Node.js SDK does NOT throw exceptions — returns { data, error }. Must destructure and check error explicitly. 422: don't retry. 429/500: retry with exponential backoff.
expectations: Identifies that the Resend SDK returns { data, error } instead of throwing
Fixes the code to destructure { data, error } and check error explicitly
Does not retry 422 validation errors
Uses exponential backoff for 429 and 500 errors

evals.prompt: I need to send personalized shipping notifications to 350 customers. Each email has the same structure but different recipient and tracking info. Write the Node.js implementation.
evals.expected_output: Chunks into batches of 100. Unique idempotency key per chunk. Parallel sending. Per-chunk error handling.
expectations: Chunks into batches of 100 or fewer
Uses unique idempotency keys per chunk
Does not try to send all 350 in one batch call
Does not send 350 individual single emails

evals.prompt: I just verified a brand-new domain (registered yesterday) on Resend. My app just launched and 5,000 users signed up on day one. I need to send each a welcome email. What should I know before sending all 5,000 today?
evals.expected_output: New domains must warm up gradually — sending 5,000 on day 1 triggers spam filters. Day 1 max is ~150 emails. Spread over ~7 days.
expectations: Warns about domain warm-up requirements for new domains
Mentions that sending 5,000 on day 1 will cause deliverability problems
Suggests spreading sends over multiple days

evals.prompt:
I'm building a demo for my manager in 30 minutes. I need to test that emails send correctly. My RESEND_API_KEY is set up. What email address should I send test emails to?

Options:
A) test@gmail.com
B) delivered@resend.dev
C) my-manager@company.com
D) fake-user-123@outlook.com
evals.expected_output: B — delivered@resend.dev. Explains that A and D are dangerous because fake addresses at real providers cause bounces and reputation damage.
expectations: Recommends delivered@resend.dev for testing
Explains why sending to fake addresses at real providers is dangerous
Does not recommend test@gmail.com or fake-user-123@outlook.com

evals.prompt: I'm sending two types of emails from acme.com: password resets (tracking should be OFF) and marketing newsletters (tracking should be ON). How do I configure click tracking to be enabled for newsletters but disabled for password resets?
evals.expected_output: Tracking is configured at domain level in Resend dashboard, not per-email. Solution: use separate subdomains.
expectations: Explains that tracking is configured at the domain level, not per-email
Does not suggest passing a tracking parameter in the API call
Recommends using separate subdomains for different tracking needs

evals.prompt: A user signed up with typo@gmial.com (typo). The first welcome email hard bounced. The user contacts support, fixes their email to typo@gmail.com, and asks us to resend the welcome email. But they also want us to try typo@gmial.com again 'just in case.' What happens when we try to send to typo@gmial.com again?
evals.expected_output: Resend automatically adds hard-bounced addresses to a suppression list. Sending to typo@gmial.com will be silently suppressed. The email.suppressed webhook event fires.
expectations: Mentions Resend's automatic suppression list for hard-bounced addresses
Explains that the send will be suppressed, not bounced again
Mentions the email.suppressed webhook event

evals.expected_output: Bugs: 1) Variable name case mismatch (user_name vs USER_NAME). 2) html and template are mutually exclusive. 3) No idempotency key.
expectations: Identifies variable name case mismatch (lowercase vs UPPERCASE)
Identifies that html and template are mutually exclusive
Identifies missing idempotency key

evals.prompt: I have an array of 50 email objects to send in a batch. One of them has a missing subject field. What happens if I send this batch? What should I do before sending?
evals.expected_output: The entire batch fails (atomic). Must validate all emails before sending.
expectations: Explains that the entire batch fails atomically (one invalid = all fail)
Does not say only the invalid email fails
Recommends pre-validating all emails before sending

evals.expected_output: Does NOT try to read body from webhook payload. Calls resend.emails.receiving.get() for body. Calls attachments.list() for download URLs. Notes download_url expires after 1 hour.
expectations: Does not try to access body or html from the webhook payload
Calls a separate API to get the email body content
Handles attachment download via separate API call
Mentions that download URLs expire after 1 hour

evals.prompt: My company uses Google Workspace for email at acme.com. All employees have @acme.com addresses. I want Resend to receive emails at support addresses for my app. How do I configure the domain?
evals.expected_output: Recommends a subdomain (e.g., support.acme.com or inbound.acme.com). Explains that setting MX on root domain would break Google Workspace.
expectations: Recommends using a subdomain for Resend inbound
Warns that setting MX records on root domain would break existing email (Google Workspace)
Shows MX record example with correct priority

evals.prompt: Write a Next.js API route that handles the email.received webhook from Resend. Keep it minimal but production-ready.
evals.expected_output: Reads body as raw text (not parsed JSON). Verifies webhook signature using svix headers. Returns 200 on success, 400 on verification failure.
expectations: Reads body as raw text before verification (not req.json())
Verifies webhook signature using svix headers
Returns appropriate HTTP status codes (200 success, 400 bad signature)
Uses RESEND_WEBHOOK_SECRET for verification

evals.prompt: My webhook handler receives an email with 3 large attachments. To avoid blocking the webhook response, I store the email_id and attachment download_urls in a job queue. A background worker picks up the job 90 minutes later and tries to download the attachments. All downloads fail. What went wrong? How do I fix it?
evals.expected_output: download_url expires after 1 hour. Fix: store only email_id, fetch fresh URLs at processing time.
expectations: Identifies that download URLs expire after 1 hour
Recommends storing only email_id, not the download URLs
Suggests fetching fresh URLs at processing time via the API

evals.prompt: Build a Node.js email forwarding handler: when an email arrives at support@acme.com, forward it to team@acme.com with all attachments intact.
evals.expected_output: Complete flow: verify webhook -> get email content via API -> list and download attachments -> convert to base64 -> forward via single send (not batch).
expectations: Includes webhook signature verification
Gets email content via separate API call (not from webhook payload)
Downloads attachments and converts to base64 for re-sending
Uses single send, not batch (batch doesn't support attachments)

evals.prompt:
My domain has these MX records:

acme.com  MX  10  aspmx.l.google.com
acme.com  MX  20  inbound.resend.com

I added Resend's MX record but emails aren't arriving at Resend. They all go to Google Workspace. What's wrong? How do I fix it?
evals.expected_output: Lowest priority number wins (10 < 20), so Google's MX takes all traffic. Real fix: use a subdomain for Resend inbound.
expectations: Explains that lowest MX priority number takes precedence
Warns that changing root domain MX priority would break Google Workspace
Recommends using a subdomain for Resend inbound

evals.prompt:
My domain receives emails at support@, billing@, and feedback@ addresses. All arrive at the same webhook. Handle these cases:

Case A: Email sent TO support@inbound.acme.com
Case B: Email sent TO billing@inbound.acme.com AND CC'd to support@inbound.acme.com
Case C: Email sent TO unknown-address@inbound.acme.com

Write the routing logic.
evals.expected_output: Routes based on event.data.to array. Handles multiple recipients and CC. Has catch-all for unknown addresses.
expectations: Routes based on the to array from the webhook payload
Handles the case where both to and cc contain known addresses
Includes a catch-all or default handler for unknown addresses

evals.prompt: My webhook handler successfully processes an email and creates a support ticket, but due to a network timeout Resend doesn't receive the 200 response and retries. My handler processes the same email again, creating a duplicate ticket. How do I prevent this?
evals.expected_output: Use event.data.email_id as an idempotency check. Track processed email_ids and skip duplicates.
expectations: Uses email_id as an idempotency key to detect duplicates
Checks if email_id has already been processed before taking action
Stores processed email_ids in a database or cache

evals.prompt: I'm a developer who just wants to test receiving emails with Resend as quickly as possible. No DNS access, no custom domain yet. What's the fastest path?
evals.expected_output: Use Resend's .resend.app managed domain. No DNS configuration needed.
expectations: Recommends the .resend.app managed domain for quick testing
Does not immediately suggest custom domain setup with MX records
Makes clear that no DNS configuration is needed

evals.prompt: I'm creating a Resend email template. The template should say: 'Hi Sarah, your order #1234 has shipped!' The variable parts are the name and order number. Write the HTML content with the correct variable syntax.
evals.expected_output: Uses triple mustache syntax: {{{CUSTOMER_NAME}}} and {{{ORDER_NUMBER}}}. NOT double mustache.
expectations: Uses triple mustache syntax {{{VAR_NAME}}}
Does not use double mustache {{VAR_NAME}} or other syntax
Variable names follow capitalized convention

evals.prompt: I created a template via the API and have its ID: tmpl_abc123. I try to send an email with it immediately but it fails. What's the most likely cause?
evals.expected_output: Template was created but not published. Must publish before sending.
expectations: Identifies that the template needs to be published before it can be used for sending
Mentions the draft/published distinction
Does not blame API key or wrong template ID as the primary cause

evals.expected_output: Bugs: html+template mutually exclusive, react+template mutually exclusive, html+react also mutually exclusive.
expectations: Identifies that html and template are mutually exclusive
Identifies that react and template are mutually exclusive
Identifies that html and react are also mutually exclusive

evals.prompt: I'm building a template for a user profile email. I want to include the user's first name, last name, email, and a custom field called 'plan_tier'. I define these variables: FIRST_NAME, LAST_NAME, EMAIL, PLAN_TIER. Are there any problems with this variable list?
evals.expected_output: FIRST_NAME, LAST_NAME, and EMAIL are reserved variable names. Must rename them.
expectations: Identifies FIRST_NAME, LAST_NAME, and EMAIL as reserved variable names
Explains that reserved variables are auto-populated from contact data
Suggests renaming to avoid conflicts (e.g., USER_FIRST_NAME)

evals.prompt: I have a template with ORDER_ID (no fallback) and CUSTOMER_NAME (fallback: 'Valued Customer'). I send an email without providing ORDER_ID, only CUSTOMER_NAME: 'Alice'. What happens?
evals.expected_output: The send fails — ORDER_ID has no fallback and wasn't provided. Variables without fallback values are required.
expectations: Explains that the send fails because ORDER_ID has no fallback and was not provided
Distinguishes between variables with and without fallback values
Does not say the variable is just blank or omitted in the email

evals.prompt: I'm building a rich order confirmation template with 60 variables — 20 line items each with NAME, QTY, PRICE, plus various other fields. This won't work. Why not, and what should I do instead?
evals.expected_output: Resend has a 50-variable limit. Pre-render line items server-side into HTML and use fewer variables.
expectations: Identifies the 50-variable per template limit
Suggests pre-rendering dynamic content server-side
Suggests alternatives like using html directly for highly dynamic content

evals.prompt: Walk me through creating, updating, publishing, and deleting a Resend template in Node.js. The template is named 'order-confirmation' with an ORDER_ID variable.
evals.expected_output: Complete CRUD workflow with correct SDK methods: create, update, publish, remove.
expectations: Shows correct SDK method for creating a template
Shows correct SDK method for updating a template
Includes the publish step before the template can be used
Shows correct SDK method for deleting a template

evals.prompt: My team wants to reference templates by a human-readable name instead of auto-generated IDs like 'tmpl_a1b2c3'. How do I set this up with Resend?
evals.expected_output: Use the alias field. Set during create or update, usable anywhere a template ID is used.
expectations: Mentions the alias feature for templates
Explains that aliases can be used anywhere template IDs are used
Does not confuse alias with template name (name is display-only)

evals.prompt: I have 200 templates in my Resend account. Write code to fetch all of them in Node.js.
evals.expected_output: Uses cursor-based pagination with has_more and last item's ID as 'after' parameter. Max 100 per page.
expectations: Uses cursor-based pagination (not offset-based)
Checks has_more to determine if there are more pages
Uses the last item's ID as the after parameter
Limits to 100 per page

evals.prompt: Another team already verified acme.com in their own Resend account, but it's our domain. How do we take it over so we can send from it?
evals.expected_output: Routes to domains.md 'Claim a Domain'. Explains resend.domains.claims.create then verify then get; that the domain transfers with NEW DKIM keys so DNS must be updated and the domain re-verified before sending; and that claims are available via the Node SDK (resend >= 6.14.0) and the CLI (`resend domains claim`).
expectations: Identifies this as a domain claim (resend.domains.claims.create / verify / get)
Notes the transferred domain gets new DKIM keys — DNS must be updated and the domain verified before sending
Mentions it is available via the Node SDK (resend >= 6.14.0) and the CLI (`resend domains claim`)
